name: Algiers Attic Radio Station
description: A residential attic on the Algiers west bank, 108 degrees at noon and not measurably cooler now; the amateur radio rig sits on a card table surrounded by yellow legal pads covered in voltage readings, triage figures, and transmission logs, its carrier wave audible as a faint hum against the heat.
atmosphericDetails: The voltmeter reads 11.8 on the yellow legal pad — the number has been written eleven times in the same column, each entry with a timestamp
The carrier wave has a barely perceptible flattening — the crystal losing its temperature compensation, the drift that means the twenty minutes that are not available are already running
Two triage sheets on the card table, same address on Flood Street, two different casualty counts; neither is annotated with a correction timestamp
The attic window is propped open with a piece of broken conduit; the air that comes through it is the same temperature as the air inside
The rooftop mirror array is accessible through the window but requires leaning out over a twelve-foot drop to the porch roof below
